commandsRun.observation: Targeted tracking backend test run exited 0. Repo inspection shows the current regression coverage lives under `TestRunningTimerConflictAcrossWorkspaces`, which proves the service rejects a second cross-workspace running timer, preserves the original current timer, and persists no second running entry.

assertions.reason: `apps/backend/internal/tracking/application/regressions_test.go` contains `TestRunningTimerConflictAcrossWorkspaces`, which verifies a second running timer in another workspace is rejected with `ErrRunningTimeEntryExists`, `GetCurrentTimeEntry` remains pinned to the original running entry, workspace A still has only the original running entry, and workspace B has zero persisted entries after the rejected attempt.

frictions.description: The ad-hoc go test command pattern referenced older test names that no longer exist verbatim in the repo.
frictions.resolution: Cross-checked the current repo test names and used file inspection to map the passing backend proof to `TestRunningTimerConflictAcrossWorkspaces`.

summary: Backend-truth coverage for the global single-running-timer invariant is present and passes for the timer-core milestone.
